rationale: The cited paper focuses on sender-side determinants of positive WOM (need for uniqueness, public/private products, recommendation vs details), while the citing paper studies receiver-side evaluation of online reviews (negativity bias, temporal contiguity cues, attribution), so the overlap appears broad rather than clearly methodological or mechanistic. However, the matched evidence is only a DOI entry with no in-text citation context, so there is not enough evidence to safely classify this as an obvious mention-only citation.
